---
title: "Skills scaffolding"
subtitle: "Where each transferable skill is introduced, practised, and expected across the four-year arc"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Scaffold placeholder
Skills are stored in `skills/skills_vocab.yml` (the controlled
vocabulary) and `skills/course_tags.yml` (per-course tagging with
introduce/practise/expect markers). Neither file exists yet. The
heatmap viz on this page is the eventual rendering of those YAMLs.
:::

# The idea

Skills get **scaffolded** through a degree: introduced in one course,
practised in several, and expected as background in upper-year courses.
Without an explicit map, neither students nor instructors can see this
arc clearly.

This page shows the scaffolded coverage as a heatmap: rows are skills
from a controlled vocabulary, columns are the four years of the
program, cells are coloured by introduce / practise / expect status.

# Controlled skill vocabulary (draft)

Three top-level skill families to start, each with sub-skills. These
are stubs; the real vocabulary will be co-developed with department
instructors.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-md-4 .g-col-12}

### Quantitative / computational
- R basics
- R intermediate (tidyverse)
- R for hydrology (tidyhydat, dataRetrieval)
- Statistical inference
- Geostatistics
- Spatial analysis (sf, terra)
- Modelling (GLM, GAM)

:::

::: {.g-col-md-4 .g-col-12}

### Field / lab
- Sampling design
- Water-quality sampling
- Macroinvertebrate identification
- Hydrometric measurement
- Lab safety
- Calibration and QC

:::

::: {.g-col-md-4 .g-col-12}

### Communication / professional
- Scientific writing
- Public-facing communication
- Peer review
- Project management
- Ethics
- Working with First Nations

:::

:::

# Pending content

- [ ] Co-develop the controlled skill vocabulary with the program
      instructor cohort.
- [ ] Tag each Freshwater Science course with the skills it
      introduces / practises / expects.
- [ ] Render the skills-by-year heatmap.
